title: Machaeranthera townshendii
taxonomy: Machaeranthera townshendii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States, specifically in the states of California and Nevada. The plant is often classified as a member of the genus Machaeranthera, which is part of the tribe Astereae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaves and flower structure. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is closely related to other plants like sunflowers and daisies.

intro: Machaeranthera townshendii, also known as Townshend's tansy aster, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States, characterized by its clusters of small, yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce pain and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Machaeranthera townshendii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
